title: Bump GoLang for CVE-2019-17596
body:
Bump golang to 1.12.12 and 1.13.X to avoid security issues
noted in CVE-2019-17596. See: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/golang-announce/lVEm7llp0w0/VbafyRkgCgAJ and https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2019-17596

I've dropped 1.11 compiles and added 1.13.X while we're at it.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@vrothberg  or @nalind.  I've added go 1.13.x to the travis build for the first time and I'm getting a tree dirty error?    Any thoughts on how to make it happy?

```
go version go1.13.3 linux/amd64
go.env

$ go env
$ make vendor
$ ./hack/tree_status.sh
tree is dirty, please commit all changes and sync the vendor.conf
```

Yes I think
make vendor-in-container 
is the way forward.  Then at least it is easy to get contributors to do the work.
The problem would be can we do this all in Buildah, or would we be requiring buildah contributors to install podman.

> Yes I think
> make vendor-in-container
> is the way forward. Then at least it is easy to get contributors to do the work.
> The problem would be can we do this all in Buildah, or would we be requiring buildah contributors to install podman.

I'd default to use `podman` but we could make it configurable with a `CTR_ENGINE` env variable. If users want to use buildah, they could do that on their own but it's not as straight-forward doing a `podman run ...`.

We need make vendor-in-container PR to fix this up.

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1949
